heading: Suspension of construction in case of treaty
label: 8664 Suspension of construction in case of treaty
text: In case of a treaty for the limitation of naval armament to which the United States is a signatory, the President may suspend so much of the authorized naval construction as is necessary to bring the naval vessels of the United States within the limitations agreed upon. Such a suspension does not apply to vessels under construction at the time the suspension is made.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)7294(as applicable to vessels).34 U.S.C. 498h(as applicable to vessels), .May 17, 1938, ch. 243, § 952 Stat. 403The words “the United States would welcome and support an international conference for naval limitations” are omitted as a declaration of purpose without permanent or general significance. The word “further” is omitted since there is no such agreement in existence today. The word “international” is omitted as unnecessary since the word “treaty” necessarily involves an international understanding. The word “may” is substituted for the words “is hereby authorized and empowered to” for brevity.
Amendments2018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 7294 of this title
